epic_summary: Develop secure, real-time data handling with resilient uptime and advanced analytics for property operations.
source_section: I. Core Product Features - A. Performance and Data Management

user_stories.user_role: As a System Administrator, I want redundant failover and uptime guarantees so that property operations continue during outages.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a node failure in the primary cluster, WHEN traffic is rerouted, THEN the platform must sustain 99.999% uptime without user-visible errors.
GIVEN scheduled maintenance, WHEN the system switches to secondary nodes, THEN active sessions must persist without forced logouts.
GIVEN sustained peak load at 95% capacity, WHEN monitoring evaluates system health, THEN auto-scaling must provision additional instances within 60 seconds.
GIVEN a failover event, WHEN logs are reviewed, THEN replication lag between nodes must remain under 2 seconds.

user_stories.user_role: As a Data Engineer, I want real-time data synchronization across services so that dashboards reflect current transactions.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a new rent payment is recorded, WHEN the reporting dashboard is refreshed, THEN the data latency must not exceed 1 second end-to-end.
GIVEN bi-directional sync is active between microservices, WHEN concurrent writes occur, THEN conflict resolution must complete within 500 ms with audit logs retained.
GIVEN network partition events, WHEN connectivity is restored, THEN the system must reconcile queued events without data loss or duplication.
GIVEN data synchronization jobs, WHEN they run under peak loads, THEN CPU and memory consumption must remain below predefined thresholds (e.g., 80% utilization).

user_stories.story_title: Enforce Secure Storage and Historical Archiving
user_stories.user_role: As a Security Officer, I want encrypted storage and archival policies so that tenant and transaction data meet compliance standards.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN data is written to cloud storage, WHEN the write completes, THEN encryption at rest must use AES-256 with keys managed via the enterprise KMS.
GIVEN archived records older than the configurable retention period, WHEN archival jobs execute, THEN records must move to cold storage with retrieval SLAs defined.
GIVEN a request to access historical data, WHEN an authorized user queries the archive, THEN results must return within 5 seconds for a 1-year range.
GIVEN backup verification runs nightly, WHEN integrity checks complete, THEN checksum validation must report zero corrupted artifacts.

user_stories.story_title: Provide Customizable Dashboards and Predictive Analytics
user_stories.user_role: As a Portfolio Manager, I want configurable dashboards with predictive analytics so that I can identify trends and forecast revenue.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user customizes a dashboard widget layout, WHEN they save the configuration, THEN the layout must persist across sessions and devices.
GIVEN analytics datasets are updated hourly, WHEN predictive models run, THEN forecasts for occupancy and rent collection must refresh within 10 minutes of data availability.
GIVEN a dashboard filter is applied (e.g., property region), WHEN the user views KPIs, THEN results must reflect the filter across all widgets without page reloads.
GIVEN model performance monitoring, WHEN drift exceeds predefined thresholds, THEN alerts must be raised and the model must fall back to the last validated version.

epic_summary: Deliver robust APIs and connectors that enable seamless data exchange with ERP, CRM, and productivity ecosystems.
source_section: I. Core Product Features - B. Integration and Compatibility

user_stories.story_title: Publish RESTful and SOAP APIs with Documentation
user_stories.user_role: As an Integrations Engineer, I want standardized REST and SOAP APIs so that third-party systems can reliably exchange data.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an API consumer with valid credentials, WHEN invoking REST endpoints, THEN responses must adhere to documented schemas with versioning headers present.
GIVEN SOAP integrations, WHEN a WSDL is requested, THEN the service must return a valid WSDL reflecting all available operations and types.
GIVEN API rate limits of 1000 requests per minute per client, WHEN limits are exceeded, THEN the system must return HTTP 429 with retry-after guidance.
GIVEN OpenAPI/Swagger documentation, WHEN the docs are generated, THEN they must reflect the latest deployed endpoints and be accessible via a docs URL.

user_stories.story_title: Provide Pre-Built Connectors for ERP, CRM, and Productivity Suites
user_stories.user_role: As an Implementation Specialist, I want certified connectors for major ERP/CRM/productivity platforms so that deployments avoid custom builds.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a supported ERP (e.g., SAP/Oracle), WHEN the connector is configured with credentials, THEN bidirectional data sync must succeed for master data and transactions.
GIVEN a CRM connector (e.g., Salesforce), WHEN lead or tenant data changes, THEN updates must propagate within 2 minutes with error retries logged.
GIVEN productivity suite integration (e.g., Microsoft 365/Google Workspace), WHEN calendar events are synced, THEN meeting invites must reflect property-related metadata fields.
GIVEN connector health monitoring, WHEN an integration error occurs, THEN alerts must be generated with actionable error codes and retry steps.

user_stories.user_role: As a DevOps Engineer, I want compatibility testing across heterogeneous IT environments so that integrations minimize deployment friction.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN on-premises and cloud environments, WHEN connectors are deployed, THEN installation scripts must complete without manual dependency resolution steps.
GIVEN multiple identity providers (e.g., SAML/OAuth), WHEN API authentication is configured, THEN SSO must function with role-based access enforced.
GIVEN network segregation policies, WHEN integrations are tested, THEN required ports/protocols must be documented and validated via automated smoke tests.

epic_summary: Ensure intuitive multi-device UX with multilingual support and WCAG 2.1 compliance for diverse user bases.
source_section: I. Core Product Features - C. User Experience (UX) and Accessibility

user_stories.user_role: As a Global Admin, I want multi-language UI support so that regional teams can operate in their preferred language.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN localization packs for supported languages, WHEN a user selects a language, THEN all UI strings and date/number formats must render accordingly without fallback placeholders.
GIVEN new feature releases, WHEN translations are updated, THEN untranslated strings must default to English with a flagged backlog item created automatically.
GIVEN role-based permissions, WHEN language settings are changed, THEN the preference must persist per user profile across web, mobile, and terminal clients.

user_stories.user_role: As an Accessibility Tester, I want WCAG 2.1 compliant interfaces so that all users can navigate and operate the system.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN UI components, WHEN evaluated with automated accessibility scanners, THEN contrast ratios, ARIA labels, and focus order must meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.
GIVEN keyboard-only navigation, WHEN users traverse forms and dashboards, THEN all interactive elements must be reachable and actionable without a mouse.
GIVEN screen reader usage, WHEN a page loads, THEN headings, landmarks, and dynamic notifications must be announced with appropriate semantics.
GIVEN accessibility regression tests, WHEN new UI components are added, THEN CI pipelines must block merges if WCAG violations are detected.

user_stories.user_role: As a Product Designer, I want consistent responsive layouts so that users on desktop, mobile, and terminals have a clear experience.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN viewport breakpoints for desktop, tablet, mobile, and terminal screens, WHEN pages are rendered, THEN layout components must adapt without horizontal scrolling.
GIVEN offline-capable mobile clients, WHEN connectivity is lost, THEN critical workflows (e.g., inspections) must allow data capture with queued sync on reconnection.
GIVEN device-specific performance budgets, WHEN pages load, THEN time-to-interactive must stay under defined thresholds (e.g., <3s on 4G mobile).

epic_summary: Provide tiered implementation packages with migration, configuration auditing, and phased rollout playbooks.
source_section: II. Professional Services and Support - A. Implementation and Deployment

user_stories.user_role: As a Professional Services Manager, I want standard and custom implementation playbooks so that customers can select suitable deployment paths.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN standard and custom package definitions, WHEN a proposal is generated, THEN scope, timelines, and deliverables must auto-populate from the selected playbook.
GIVEN an expedited setup request, WHEN the package is applied, THEN the system must validate prerequisite readiness (network, identity, data sources) before scheduling.
GIVEN bespoke requirements, WHEN a custom package is configured, THEN change approvals must be version-controlled with stakeholder sign-off captured.

user_stories.story_title: Execute Legacy Data Migration and Configuration Audits
user_stories.user_role: As a Migration Lead, I want audited migration workflows so that legacy property data imports reliably into the new platform.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN legacy datasets, WHEN migration ETL runs, THEN field mappings and transformations must be validated against a predefined schema with error reports generated for anomalies.
GIVEN configuration baselines, WHEN audits are executed, THEN deviations (e.g., permission mismatches) must be reported with remediation steps before go-live.
GIVEN migration dry-runs, WHEN results are reviewed, THEN success criteria must include zero critical errors and less than 0.5% non-critical warnings before production cutover.
GIVEN phased rollout strategy, WHEN phase gates are reached, THEN go/no-go checklists must be signed off by both services and customer stakeholders.

epic_summary: Deliver role-based training, certification paths, and a searchable knowledge base for continuous user proficiency.
source_section: II. Professional Services and Support - B. Training and Knowledge Transfer

user_stories.user_role: As a Training Coordinator, I want separate curricula for Administrators, Power Users, and End Users so that each role gains relevant proficiency.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN defined roles, WHEN a learner enrolls, THEN the LMS must assign the appropriate curriculum with mandatory modules per role.
GIVEN completion tracking, WHEN a learner finishes required modules, THEN certificates must generate automatically and be exportable for audits.
GIVEN feedback collection, WHEN a course ends, THEN surveys must trigger and results stored for quality reporting.
GIVEN recurring releases, WHEN new features launch, THEN refresher modules must be added to role curricula within two weeks.

user_stories.story_title: Deliver Multi-Modal Training and Knowledge Base Access
user_stories.user_role: As a Product Trainer, I want on-site workshops, certified online courses, and a searchable knowledge base so that users can choose their preferred learning mode.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN on-site workshop scheduling, WHEN dates are confirmed, THEN logistics (location, capacity, materials) must auto-generate and sync to calendars.
GIVEN online course delivery, WHEN a learner starts a module, THEN streaming and interactive labs must load within 3 seconds with progress saved automatically.
GIVEN the knowledge base, WHEN a user searches by keyword, THEN relevant articles, tutorials, and FAQs must return ranked by usage analytics within 1 second.
GIVEN access control, WHEN external partners log in, THEN knowledge base visibility must honor entitlements and redact internal-only content.

epic_summary: Provide tiered support with proactive monitoring, automated patches, and premium SLAs for rapid incident resolution.
source_section: II. Professional Services and Support - C. Technical Support and Maintenance

user_stories.story_title: Deliver Standard Support with 24/7 Monitoring and Auto-Patching
user_stories.user_role: As a Support Engineer, I want continuous monitoring and automatic patch deployment so that standard customers stay secure and stable.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN 24/7 monitoring, WHEN critical alerts trigger, THEN on-call rotations must be notified within 2 minutes with incident tickets auto-created.
GIVEN patch releases, WHEN maintenance windows occur, THEN patches must auto-deploy with rollback plans and success verification logs.
GIVEN monitoring dashboards, WHEN metrics breach defined SLAs, THEN escalation policies must execute with documented runbooks.

user_stories.story_title: Provide Premium SLA with Prioritized Access and Proactive Health Checks
user_stories.user_role: As an Account Manager, I want premium customers to receive prioritized expert access and proactive health checks so that incidents are resolved rapidly and issues are prevented.
acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a premium customer incident, WHEN a ticket is logged, THEN a technical specialist must respond within the contracted SLA (e.g., 15 minutes) with escalation paths defined.
GIVEN dedicated account assignments, WHEN quarterly reviews occur, THEN health check reports with remediation plans must be delivered and acknowledged by the customer.
GIVEN proactive system health checks, WHEN potential risks are detected (e.g., capacity nearing limits), THEN preventive actions must be documented and scheduled before impact.
GIVEN premium communication channels, WHEN customers call or chat, THEN priority routing must connect them to senior engineers without queue delays.
